volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 970 - Section 1.1095)

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: ὅσαι δʼ Ἐρινύων οὐκ ἐπείσθησαν νόμῳ ,
sections.translation: but others were not persuaded by the law , and drove me always in vagabond courses until I came to the holy plain of Phoebus in turn . Stretched out before his shrine and fasting , I swore to break off my life and die there ,

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: εἰ μή με σώσει Φοῖβος , ὅς μʼ ἀπώλεσεν .
sections.translation: if Phoebus , who had destroyed me , did not save me . And then Phoebus cried out a golden voice from the tripod , and sent me here , to get the image Zeus hurled down , and set it up in Athena ’ s land . But what he marked out for my safety

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: σύμπραξον · ἢν γὰρ θεᾶς κατάσχωμεν βρέτας ,
sections.translation: you must help me with ; for if we possess the statue of the goddess , I will be released from madness and will put you on my ship of many oars and establish you again in Mycenae . But , my beloved sister , save our father ’ s house and save me ;

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: ὡς τἄμʼ ὄλωλε πάντα καὶ τὰ Πελοπιδῶν ,
sections.translation: for so I perish and all the race of Pelops , unless we take the heavenly image of the goddess .

sections.label: Section 987
sections.original: δεινή τις ὀργὴ δαιμόνων ἐπέζεσε
sections.translation: A terrible anger from the gods has boiled up against the race of Tantalus and drives them through torments .

sections.label: Section 989
sections.original: τὸ μὲν πρόθυμον , πρίν σε δεῦρʼ ἐλθεῖν , ἔχω
sections.translation: Before you came here , I was eager

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: Ἄργει γενέσθαι καὶ σέ , σύγγονʼ , εἰσιδεῖν .
sections.translation: to be in Argos and see you , my brother . Your wish is mine : to release you from torment , and restore our father ’ s afflicted house , for I am not angry at the one who killed me ; it is my wish . I would set my hand free from your slaughter

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: σῴσαιμί τʼ οἴκους . τὴν θεὸν δʼ ὅπως λάθω
sections.translation: and save our house . But I worry about concealment from the goddess and the king , when he finds the stone pedestal empty of the statue . How will I escape death ? What argument will I have ? But if this one thing happens all together ,

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ἄγαλμά τʼ οἴσεις κἄμʼ ἐπʼ εὐπρύμνου νεὼς
sections.translation: that you take the statue and bear me away on your lovely ship , the venture is a noble one . If I am separated from this , I am lost , but you might settle your affairs well and have a safe return . Indeed , I do not shrink from it , not even if I must die

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: σῴσασαν · οὐ γὰρ ἀλλʼ ἀνὴρ μὲν ἐκ δόμων
sections.translation: to save you . No , for when a man dies and is gone from the home , he is longed for ; but women are powerless .

sections.label: Section 1007
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν γενοίμην σοῦ τε καὶ μητρὸς φονεύς ·
sections.translation: I would not be the murderer of you as well as my mother ; her blood is enough ; I would rather have an equal share of life or death , in common with you .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ἄξω δέ γʼ , ἤνπερ καὐτὸς ἐνταυθοῖ περῶ ,
sections.translation: I will bring you home , if I myself escape from here , or if I die , I will remain here with you . Listen to what I think : if Artemis were hostile to this , how could Loxias have prophesied that I would take the statue of the goddess to Pallas ’ city . . .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: καὶ σὸν πρόσωπον εἰσιδεῖν ; ἅπαντα γὰρ
sections.translation: and see your face . Putting all these things together , I have hope of our return .

sections.label: Section 1017
sections.original: πῶς οὖν γένοιτʼ ἂν ὥστε μήθʼ ἡμᾶς θανεῖν ,
sections.translation: But how may we live , and take what we want ? For our return home suffers from this ; but the will is present .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἂν τύραννον διολέσαι δυναίμεθʼ ἄν ;
sections.translation: Could we murder the king ?

sections.label: Section 1021
sections.original: δεινὸν τόδʼ εἶπας , ξενοφονεῖν ἐπήλυδας .
sections.translation: A fearful suggestion , for foreigners to kill their host !

sections.label: Section 1022
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , εἰ σὲ σώσει κἀμέ , κινδυνευτέον .
sections.translation: But we must dare it , if it brings our safety .

sections.label: Section 1023
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν δυναίμην · τὸ δὲ πρόθυμον ᾔνεσα .
sections.translation: I could not ; yet I approve your eagerness .

sections.label: Section 1024
sections.original: τί δʼ , εἴ με ναῷ τῷδε κρύψειας λάθρα ;
sections.translation: What if you were to hide me secretly in the shrine ?

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ὡς δὴ σκότον λαβόντες ἐκσωθεῖμεν ἄν ;
sections.translation: So that we might take advantage of the darkness and escape ?

sections.label: Section 1026
sections.original: κλεπτῶν γὰρ ἡ νύξ , τῆς δʼ ἀληθείας τὸ φῶς .
sections.translation: Yes , for the night belongs to thieves , the light to truth .

sections.label: Section 1027
sections.original: εἴσʼ ἔνδον ἱεροὶ φύλακες , οὓς οὐ λήσομεν .
sections.translation: There are secred guards within , who will notice us .

sections.label: Section 1028
sections.original: οἴμοι , διεφθάρμεσθα · πῶς σωθεῖμεν ἄν ;
sections.translation: Alas , we are ruined ! How can we be saved ?

sections.label: Section 1029
sections.original: ἔχειν δοκῶ μοι καινὸν ἐξεύρημά τι .
sections.translation: I think I have a new stratagem .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ποῖόν τι ; δόξης μετάδος , ὡς κἀγὼ μάθω .
sections.translation: What is it ? Let me know ; share your thought .

sections.label: Section 1031
sections.original: ταῖς σαῖς ἀνίαις χρήσομαι σοφίσμασι .
sections.translation: I will use your sorrows as my contrivance .

sections.label: Section 1032
sections.original: δειναὶ γὰρ αἱ γυναῖκες εὑρίσκειν τέχνας .
sections.translation: Women are wonderfully good at devising crafty plans !

sections.label: Section 1033
sections.original: φονέα σε φήσω μητρὸς ἐξ Ἄργους μολεῖν .
sections.translation: I will say that you came from Argos after killing your mother .

sections.label: Section 1034
sections.original: χρῆσαι κακοῖσι τοῖς ἐμοῖς , εἰ κερδανεῖς .
sections.translation: Make use of my troubles , if you gain by it .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ὡς οὐ θέμις γε λέξομεν θύειν θεᾷ ,
sections.translation: And that it is not right to sacrifice you to the goddess .

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: τίνʼ αἰτίαν ἔχουσʼ ; ὑποπτεύω τι γάρ .
sections.translation: With what reason ? I have a suspicion .

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: οὐ καθαρὸν ὄντα · τὸ δʼ ὅσιον δώσω φόβῳ .
sections.translation: Because you are not pure ; I will frighten what is sacred .

sections.label: Section 1038
sections.original: τί δῆτα μᾶλλον θεᾶς ἄγαλμʼ ἁλίσκεται ;
sections.translation: How does this help us to seize the statue of the goddess ?

sections.label: Section 1039
sections.original: πόντου σε πηγαῖς ἁγνίσαι βουλήσομαι ,
sections.translation: I shall want to purify you in the waves of the sea —

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: ἔτʼ ἐν δόμοισι βρέτας , ἐφʼ ᾧ πεπλεύκαμεν .
sections.translation: The image that we have sailed for is still in the temple .

sections.label: Section 1041
sections.original: κἀκεῖνο νίψαι , σοῦ θιγόντος ὥς , ἐρῶ .
sections.translation: I will say that I have washed that also , since you have touched it .

sections.label: Section 1042
sections.original: ποῖ δῆτα ; πόντου νοτερὸν εἶπας ἔκβολον ;
sections.translation: Where ? Do you mean the watery inlet of the sea ?

sections.label: Section 1043
sections.original: οὗ ναῦς χαλινοῖς λινοδέτοις ὁρμεῖ σέθεν .
sections.translation: Where your ship is moored by its roped anchor .

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἤ τις ἄλλος ἐν χεροῖν οἴσει βρέτας ;
sections.translation: Will you or some other bear the image in your hands ?

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: ἐγώ · θιγεῖν γὰρ ὅσιόν ἐστʼ ἐμοὶ μόνῃ .
sections.translation: I shall ; it is holy for me alone to touch it .

sections.label: Section 1046
sections.original: Πυλάδης δʼ ὅδʼ ἡμῖν ποῦ τετάξεται πόνου ;
sections.translation: But Pylades here — how will he be assigned to our labor ?

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: ταὐτὸν χεροῖν σοὶ λέξεται μίασμʼ ἔχων .
sections.translation: His hands , it will be said , are stained like yours with blood-pollution .

sections.label: Section 1048
sections.original: λάθρα δʼ ἄνακτος ἢ εἰδότος δράσεις τάδε ;
sections.translation: Will you do all this secretly from the king , or with his knowledge ?

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: πείσασα μύθοις · οὐ γὰρ ἂν λάθοιμί γε .
sections.translation: I shall persuade him ; I could not hide it from him .

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: καὶ μὴν νεώς γε πίτυλος εὐήρης πάρα .
sections.translation: Well then , my ship , with its quick stroke of oars , is at hand .

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: σοὶ δὴ μέλειν χρὴ τἄλλʼ ὅπως ἕξει καλῶς .
sections.translation: You must indeed take care of the rest , so that it goes well .

sections.label: Section 1052
sections.original: ἑνὸς μόνου δεῖ , τάσδε συγκρύψαι τάδε .
sections.translation: We need only one thing more , that these women conceal our plans ; you approach them and find persuasive words . A woman has power over pity .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: τὰ δʼ ἄλλʼ ἴσως — . ἅπαντα συμβαίη καλῶς .
sections.translation: The rest , perhaps —. May all turn out well !

sections.label: Section 1056
sections.original: ὦ φίλταται γυναῖκες , εἰς ὑμᾶς βλέπω ,
sections.translation: My dearest friends , I look to you ; I am in your hands , whether I am to succeed , or come to nothing and lose my country , and my dear brother and dearest sister .

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: καὶ πρῶτα μέν μοι τοῦ λόγου τάδʼ ἀρχέτω ·
sections.translation: And first of all , I begin my speech with this : we are women , and have hearts naturally formed to love each other , and keep our common interests most secure . Be silent for us and assist us in our flight . It is good to have trustworthy speech .

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: ὁρᾶτε δʼ ὡς τρεῖς μία τύχη τοὺς φιλτάτους ,
sections.translation: You see how one fortune holds us three , most dear to each other , either to return to our native land , or to die . If I am saved , I will bring you safe to Hellas , so that you may share my fortune . By your right hand , I entreat you , and you , and you ; ( addressing different members of the Chorus .) you by your dear face ,

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: γονάτων τε καὶ τῶν ἐν δόμοισι φιλτάτων
sections.translation: by your knees , by all that is dearest to you in your home : father , mother , child , if you have children . What do you reply ? Who agrees with us , or is not willing to do this — speak ! For if you do not acquiesce in my words , both I and my unhappy brother must die .

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: θάρσει , φίλη δέσποινα , καὶ σῴζου μόνον ·
sections.translation: Have courage , dear mistress , only see to your safety ; I will be silent on all that you have charged me with — great Zeus be my witness .

sections.label: Section 1078
sections.original: ὄναισθε μύθων καὶ γένοισθʼ εὐδαίμονες .
sections.translation: Bless you for your words , may you be happy ! It is your work now , and yours , To Orestes and Pylades to enter the temple ;

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ὡς αὐτίχʼ ἥξει τῆσδε κοίρανος χθονός ,
sections.translation: for soon the ruler of this land will come , inquiring if the sacrifice of the strangers has been carried out .

sections.label: Section 1082
sections.original: ὦ πότνιʼ , ἥπερ μʼ Αὐλίδος κατὰ πτυχὰς
sections.translation: Lady Artemis , you who saved me from my father ’ s slaughtering hand by the clefts of Aulis , save me now also , and these men ; or through you Loxias ’

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: οὐκέτι βροτοῖσι διὰ σὲ ἐτήτυμον στόμα .
sections.translation: prophetic voice will no longer be held true by mortals But leave this barbarian land for Athens with good will ; it is not fitting for you to dwell here , when you could have so fortunate a city . Exeunt Iphigenia , Orestes , and Pylades .

sections.label: Section 1.1089
sections.original: ὄρνις , ἃ παρὰ πετρίνας
sections.translation: Halcyon bird , you that sing your fate as a lament

sections.label: Section 1.1090
sections.original: πόντου δειράδας , ἀλκυών ,
sections.translation: beside the rocky ridges of the sea , a cry easily understood by the wise , that you are always chanting for your husband ; I , wingless bird that I am , compare

sections.label: Section 1.1095
sections.original: θρήνους , ἄπτερος ὅρνις ,
sections.translation: my laments with yours , in my longing for the festivals of Hellas , and for Artemis of childbirth , who dwells beside the Cynthian mountain and the palm with delicate leaves
